title: Lysogeny broth (LB) Media Preparation and Recipe_v1
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1ffqj","text":"Lysogeny broth (LB) media is one the most used medium in microbiology. LB broth supports Escherichia coli growth to an optical density at 600 nm (OD600) of 7. The carbon sources for E. coli in Luria-Bertani broth are catabolizable amino acids, not sugars.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":89,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":182,"length":7},{"style":"sub","offset":148,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Ensure the beaker is clean by rinsing with DI H2O
To prepare 1 L, dissolve 10 g tryptone, 5 g yeast extract, and 10 g NaCl in 0.950 L of deionized water and fill to 1 L Have a little water in the bottle before you add the dry powder.
Place autoclave tape on the lid of the bottle and autoclave at 121°C for 20-30 minutes Make sure the lid is loosened to avoid pressure build up.
Store at room temperature or in a fridge until use.
creator: Tamra Mbeuh Lahom Lot
